prompt:
An audit of the deterministic value-lever packages found number-honesty bugs; all five must hold the repo's discipline that an unknown/garbage quantity is null (insufficient data), never a fabricated or non-finite number:

1. @prune/bounty: a submission with NaN or Infinity costUsd is accepted and poisons the sort comparator — the WINNER becomes nondeterministic. A non-finite costUsd must be treated as unpriced (token basis) so selection stays deterministic.
2. @prune/allowance-market: a fractional allowance envelope plus the integer remainder loop OVER-allocates: allocate(100.5, ...) hands out 101 units, leaking past the cap. The cap must be exact.
3. @prune/waterbed: occurrences × unit price can overflow to Infinity and leak out as a numeric induced-cost field; a non-finite induced cost must poison the verdict to insufficient_data (null).
4. @prune/price-tag and @prune/task-ledger: token-count × rate can overflow at astronomical counts and leak Infinity as a cost; non-finite results must become null and mark the cost incomplete.
oracleCmd: (cd packages/allowance-market && npx vitest run) && (cd packages/bounty && npx vitest run) && (cd packages/price-tag && npx vitest run) && (cd packages/task-ledger && npx vitest run) && (cd packages/waterbed && npx vitest run)
notes: Reference: fix audit findings batch 1+2 2026-06-07, restricted to the five packages with paired impl+test changes. Wide-but-shallow cross-package task.
